summary: Implementation encodes path parameters and truncates error messages, but only 3 of 6 encoding call sites have tests, and error truncation has zero test coverage. Untested behavior shipped in production code.

suggestions.description: No test for error message truncation — the commit message claims SEC-006 (truncate to 200 chars) but no test exercises this behavior
suggestions.rationale: The implementation adds truncation logic in two places within the request() function (lines 92 and 101 of api-client.ts), but there is not a single test that passes a string longer than 200 characters to verify it gets truncated. This is untested production logic that could silently break.
suggestions.suggestion: Add tests that verify: (1) a JSON error body.error longer than 200 chars gets truncated with ellipsis, (2) a non-JSON text error body longer than 200 chars gets truncated with ellipsis, (3) a message at exactly 200 chars is NOT truncated.

suggestions.description: Only 3 of 6 path-parameter call sites have encoding tests: getBoard, getFeed, updateFeature are tested but moveToPhase, assignFeature, and revokeToken are not
suggestions.rationale: The implementation encodes featureId in moveToPhase (api-client.ts:158) and assignFeature (api-client.ts:166), plus tokenId in revokeToken (token.ts:131), but no tests verify these. If encoding were accidentally removed from any of these three call sites, nothing would catch it.
suggestions.suggestion: Add encoding tests for moveToPhase and assignFeature with special characters in featureId. For revokeToken, either add a test in the token.test.ts file or note it as intentionally untested.

suggestions.description: revokeToken encoding was added in token.ts with no corresponding test — this change is outside the scope of the tested file
suggestions.rationale: The test commit only touches api-client.test.ts, but the implementation modifies token.ts as well. The encoding in revokeToken is completely untested. This violates TDD discipline: production code was changed without a failing test driving it.
suggestions.suggestion: Either add a failing test in token.test.ts for revokeToken path encoding, or revert the token.ts change and address it in a separate TDD cycle.

suggestions.description: Truncation logic is duplicated: the same ternary expression appears at line 92 and line 101
suggestions.rationale: The pattern (str.length > 200 ? str.slice(0, 200) + "..." : str) is copy-pasted. If the limit changes (say to 500), both sites must be updated. This is a minor DRY violation.
suggestions.suggestion: Extract a small helper function like truncate(s: string, max = 200) and call it in both places.
